Tests the length property of the String prototype in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the default length property of the `String.prototype` object in JavaScript. The assertion verifies that `String.prototype.length` is equal to 0, which is the expected behavior as `String.prototype` is an empty string by default. This test ensures that the JavaScript environment adheres to the ECMAScript specification regarding the `String.prototype` object. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on validating a specific property of the `String` object prototype.
